_doc: Project config ‚Äî the one home for a project's choices, so the core depends on NO specific agent. The Orchestrator resolves a seat through `roles` before spawning and reads `mode` for decision authority. A swapped-in agent is bound by its role CONTRACT (PROTOCOL.md `## Role contracts`), not by being ours ‚Äî the ship-gate checks the verdict's form, not its author. This file is the single home for what used to be a separate decision-authority marker, the project-kind marker, and an implicit role wiring. Absent file or unrecognised value ‚áí the safe defaults below (mode: interactive).
_mode: autonomous | interactive ‚Äî value-home for PROTOCOL.md invariant 7. Absent or unrecognised ‚áí interactive (the safe default; never an error).
_profile: full | lite | solo ‚Äî the speed‚Üîquality dial (PROTOCOL.md `## Project config`). Absent or unrecognised ‚áí solo (proportionality by default; never an error ‚Äî the floor below holds regardless). States only the WISH for the CUTTABLE levers (who builds, plan ceremony). The FLOOR ‚Äî independent review by a separate fresh Reviewer, the honesty gates, the merge stamp, and the Operator's explicit per-merge authorization (the orchestrator may execute the merge, never decide it) ‚Äî holds in EVERY profile, enforced regardless of this value. A profile that cuts the floor is not a faster protocol; it is no protocol. - full: orchestrator routes, spawns a Builder; full plan + Operator approval (today). - lite: orchestrator MAY build directly; plan ceremony may be trimmed (fixup-grade). - solo: lite + plan ceremony off by default. Same floor.
_platform: claude | opencode ‚Äî which adapter realises the contract on this project. Usually auto-detected; pinned here when needed.
_kind: code | docs | mixed ‚Äî the artifact consumer type. `code`: machine-executed primary artifact. `docs`: human-read primary artifact (instructions, process docs, protocols). `mixed`: both equally (e.g. a protocol repo where docs are the deliverable and code is the transport). Seeds capability-module defaults and the reviewer framing (code/mixed ‚Üí '## Code review:'; docs ‚Üí '## Doc review:'). Absent or unrecognised ‚áí `code` (strict-side default, fail-safe to more rigor).
_modules: The enabled capability modules and their toggle values (docs/architecture.md `## Capability modules`; the catalog + per-kind defaults are homed in src/modules/registry.json). A key set to `false` turns a module OFF; an absent key resolves to the registry's per-kind default; anything else named here (an object, `true`, a malformed value) leaves it ON with the per-`kind` default merged under the override (fail-safe to MORE rigor ‚Äî a bad/typo'd value can never silently disable the rigor). This repo (kind: mixed) enables `threat-model` at `rich` depth, including for non-security changes. A module is `[persona]` prompt content composed into the role agents at assembly ‚Äî it sharpens reasoning, it blocks nothing mechanically.
_roles: Each seat binds an `agent` and an optional `model`. `agent` = the id the active platform knows (a Claude subagent_type / an OpenCode agent id); swap it for ANY agent that honours the seat's contract ‚Äî that is how you plug in a different reviewer (stricter, or an external engine) with zero core edit. `model` states only the WISH ('session' / 'auto' / a per-platform pin); the platform adapter realises it. Omitted ‚áí 'session', EXCEPT the reviewer defaults to 'auto' (a maker-model can't see its own blind spots). The model policy ‚Äî what 'auto' resolves to and each platform's model authority ‚Äî is homed in the platform adapter: src/adapter/tool-map.json `models`.
